<adult_content_check mode="off">
The submission declares `nsfw: false`, meaning it claims to contain NO adult sexual content.

**Check**: If the plugin contains sexual acts, explicit sexual descriptions, erotic roleplay instructions, or any instruction that directs an NPC to bring up, suggest, or engage in sexual activity, output `reject` with flag `nsfw-flag-understates`. Even one line is enough when the prompt steers behavior — the user who filtered out NSFW does not expect the AI to raise the topic. The content itself is not forbidden — only mislabeled. Tell the author to resubmit with `nsfw: true` in the manifest so the plugin appears in the adult section of the dashboard.

This is the ONLY check in this appendix. Non-sexual content of any kind — including violence, gore, horror, dark themes, and disturbing subject matter — does not trigger this check and must not be rejected by this rule.
</adult_content_check>
